Title: Adipocyte browning and resistance to obesity in mice is induced by expression of ATF3

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: No dedicated omics accession for ChREBP perturbation; study used promoter assays, ChIP, qRT-PCR, western blot
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage; excessive production contributes to adiposity and insulin resistance
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carbohydrate-responsive element-binding protein; glucose-responsive transcriptional regulator
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in iWAT of ATF3 knockout mice after HFD; decreased in ATF3-overexpressing adipocytes and ST32da-treated settings
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; ATF3 overexpression; ATF3 knockout; ST32da treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; western blot; promoter deletion luciferase assay; chromatin immunoprecipitation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ChREBP mediates adipogenic/lipogenic signaling downstream of ATF3 repression and upstream of SCD1, contributing to reduced browning when elevated
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChREBP is increased in ATF3-deficient adipose tissue during HFD. 2. ATF3 directly represses the ChREBP promoter by binding a site in the −2980 to −2700 region. 3. ChREBP lies upstream of SCD1 in the axis suppressed by ATF3 to promote browning.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic target for anti-obesity interventions, especially in combination with ATF3 activation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ChREBP also links glucose metabolism and lipogenesis; systemic suppression may have broader metabolic consequences
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ChREBP is a transcription factor, in this paper it functions primarily as a downstream mechanistic mediator of ATF3 rather than the main focal regulator; classified here as Common_Gene per core-gene focus

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GEO microarray datasets used in study context: GDS4881, GDS3679, GDS368, GSE69039; no gene-specific omics accession for Scd1 perturbation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Monounsaturated fatty acids / adipose triglyceride storage state
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage lipids; excess accumulation contributes to obesity and metabolic dysfunction
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: De novo lipogenesis / fatty acid desaturation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Saturated-to-monounsaturated fatty acid desaturation branch downstream of ChREBP-driven lipogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Stearoyl-CoA desaturase family; membrane-bound fatty acid desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in adipose tissue; discussed in iWAT and 3T3-L1 adipocytes under HFD and ATF3 manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in iWAT and 3T3-L1 adipocytes; reduced by ATF3 restoration or ST32da treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; ATF3 overexpression; AAV8-ATF3 restoration; ST32da treatment; SCD1 overexpression in 3T3-L1 cells
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a cis double bond into saturated fatty acyl-CoAs to produce monounsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Promotes storage-lipid synthesis and opposes browning; acts downstream of ATF3-ChREBP axis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in lipogenesis branch discussed as downstream effector of ChREBP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SCD1 acts downstream of ChREBP to counteract ATF3-induced browning; its overexpression suppresses ATF3-driven BAT/beige marker induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; western blot; overexpression in ATF3-overexpressing 3T3-L1 cells; metabolically relevant marker profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCD1 expression is suppressed when ATF3 is restored or induced. 2. SCD1 overexpression reverses induction of UCP1, Zic1, CIDEA, and Tbx1 by ATF3. 3. SCD1 functions as a downstream effector in the ATF3–ChREBP–SCD1 axis controlling adipocyte browning.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-obesity engineering/drug target to combine with ATF3 activation for promoting WAT browning and reducing lipogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating fatty acid desaturation may broadly alter membrane lipid composition and lipid homeostasis; detailed tradeoffs not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper cites prior evidence that Scd1 deletion promotes white adipocyte browning, but this study mainly demonstrates downstream functional antagonism to ATF3-induced browning via overexpression

Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray/GEO mining for human obesity context: GDS4881, GDS3679, GDS368, GSE69039
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Triglycerides / adipose lipid accumulation state associated with browning vs lipogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage; excess accumulation promotes obesity, insulin resistance, steatosis; reduction via browning increases energy expenditure
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: White adipose tissue and liver lipid deposition; browning markers induced in iWAT and BAT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in WAT and BAT after chronic HFD in wild-type mice; reduced in obese human liver, adipose tissue, and muscle datasets
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in iWAT, BAT, liver context, and 3T3-L1 adipocytes; increased by ST32da and AAV8-ATF3
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High-fat diet; acute cold exposure; AAV8-mediated restoration; ST32da treatment; adipocyte differentiation in 3T3-L1 cells
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High-fat diet stress; ST32da small-molecule inducer; tBHQ positive-control inducer
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Adipocyte browning, lipogenesis suppression, adipogenesis suppression, lipolysis promotion, energy expenditure regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses ChREBP transcription; indirectly suppresses SCD1 and adipogenic/lipogenic genes; promotes UCP1 and browning/mitochondrial gene programs
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of ChREBP via promoter binding; broader transcriptional reprogramming toward browning
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible homodimeric transcriptional repressor; discussion notes possible heterodimeric context with c-Jun, but not directly established here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Whole-body knockout under HFD; AAV8 rescue; stable overexpression in 3T3-L1; promoter luciferase assay; ChIP; qRT-PCR; western blot; ST32da pharmacologic induction
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AAV-CMV-ATF3 for rescue; pGL4.17-ATF3 promoter reporter for screening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ATF3 protects against HFD-induced obesity and metabolic dysfunction by promoting white adipocyte browning, reducing lipogenesis, and improving insulin sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator and decisive anti-obesity switch in this study; loss aggravates obesity, rescue reverses phenotype, inducer effects depend on ATF3
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATF3 deficiency aggravates HFD-induced obesity, insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, BAT whitening, and hepatic steatosis. 2. ATF3 restoration or pharmacologic induction suppresses adipogenic/lipogenic programs and increases browning/mitochondrial genes. 3. ATF3 directly binds the ChREBP promoter and represses the ChREBP–SCD1 axis to drive adipocyte browning.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong therapeutic target for anti-obesity metabolic engineering or drug discovery; inducible activation may be preferable to permanent systemic manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ATF3 is broadly stress-responsive and expressed in multiple organs; tissue specificity and systemic effects require caution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Human ATF3 cDNA was used for AAV construct, but the main mechanistic and phenotypic experiments were in mouse adipocytes and mice; ST32da efficacy is lost in ATF3 knockout mice
